pi_items.source: Verified against install_gvsig.sh which downloads to GVSIG_DATA_DIR/cities and task description.
ESRI Shapefile specification defines type 5 as Polygon. A buffer operation on the input points (type 1) produces polygons.
Python analysis of the input DBF file shows 243 records. A buffer without dissolve will yield 243 features, making a minimum bound of 100 reasonable.
ESRI Shapefile specification requires .shp, .shx, and .dbf extensions for a valid shapefile.

privileged_info_summary: The input dataset is the real Natural Earth 'ne_110m_populated_places' shapefile, which contains 243 point features (geometry type 1). The expected output is a shapefile representing buffers around these points, which necessitates a polygon geometry type (ESRI type 5). A minimum feature count of 100 is a valid lower bound given the 243 input points. The output must consist of the three mandatory shapefile components: .shp, .shx, and .dbf.
